models.code:
class Customer(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'customer'

    Id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    Name = Column(String, nullable=False)
    Balance = Column(Decimal, nullable=False)
    CreditLimit = Column(Decimal, nullable=False)
models.description: Customer details including balance and credit limit
models.name: Customer

models.code:
class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order'

    Id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    CustomerId = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('customer.Id'))
    AmountTotal = Column(Decimal, nullable=False)
    DateShipped = Column(DateTime)
    Notes = Column(String)
models.description: Orders placed by customers with related information
models.name: Order

models.code:
class Item(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'item'

    Id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    OrderId = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('order.Id'))
    ProductId = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('product.Id'))
    Quantity = Column(Integer)
    Amount = Column(Decimal, nullable=False)
    UnitPrice = Column(Decimal, nullable=False)
models.description: Items in an order with specific product and unit price details
models.name: Item

models.code:
class Product(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'product'

    Id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    Name = Column(String, nullable=False)
    UnitPrice = Column(Decimal, nullable=False)
models.name: Product

rules.name: CheckCredit1
rules.description: Ensure customer's balance is within credit limit
rules.code: Rule.constraint(validate=Customer, as_condition=lambda row: row.Balance <= row.CreditLimit, error_msg='Balance exceeds credit limit')

rules.name: CheckCredit2
rules.description: Customer balance derived from non-shipped orders sum
rules.code: Rule.sum(derive=Customer.Balance, as_sum_of=Order.AmountTotal, where=lambda row: row.DateShipped is None)

rules.name: CheckCredit3
rules.description: Order amount is sum of item amounts

rules.name: CheckCredit4
rules.description: Item amount is calculated as quantity times unit price

rules.name: CheckCredit5
rules.description: Item unit price copied from product unit price

rules.name: AppIntegration1
rules.description: Send Order data to Kafka if date_shipped is not None
rules.code: Rule.after_flush_row_event(on_class=Order, calling=kafka_producer.send_row_to_kafka, if_condition=lambda row: row.DateShipped is not None, with_args={'topic': 'order_shipping'})

test_data:
# Test Data

# Customers
customer1 = Customer(Id=1, Name="John Doe", Balance=0, CreditLimit=5000)
customer2 = Customer(Id=2, Name="Jane Smith", Balance=0, CreditLimit=10000)
customer3 = Customer(Id=3, Name="Emily Johnson", Balance=0, CreditLimit=7500)
customer4 = Customer(Id=4, Name="Mike Brown", Balance=0, CreditLimit=2000)

# Orders
order1 = Order(Id=1, CustomerId=1, AmountTotal=0, DateShipped=None)
order2 = Order(Id=2, CustomerId=2, AmountTotal=0, DateShipped=date(2023, 3, 15))
order3 = Order(Id=3, CustomerId=3, AmountTotal=0, DateShipped=None)
order4 = Order(Id=4, CustomerId=4, AmountTotal=0, DateShipped=date(2023, 5, 9))

# Items
item1 = Item(Id=1, OrderId=1, ProductId=1, Quantity=2, Amount=0, UnitPrice=20)
item2 = Item(Id=2, OrderId=2, ProductId=2, Quantity=5, Amount=0, UnitPrice=15)
item3 = Item(Id=3, OrderId=3, ProductId=3, Quantity=3, Amount=0, UnitPrice=30)
item4 = Item(Id=4, OrderId=4, ProductId=1, Quantity=1, Amount=0, UnitPrice=20)

# Products
product1 = Product(Id=1, Name="Widget A", UnitPrice=20)
product2 = Product(Id=2, Name="Widget B", UnitPrice=15)
product3 = Product(Id=3, Name="Widget C", UnitPrice=30)
product4 = Product(Id=4, Name="Widget D", UnitPrice=50)
